---
title: "161.251 Regression Modelling — Compiled Lectures"
author: "Jonathan Godfrey and Nick Knowlton"
date: "updated on `r format(Sys.Date(), '%d %B %Y')`"
documentclass: book
bibliography: [book.bib, packages.bib]
biblio-style: apalike
link-citations: yes
description: "Lecture notes for 161.251 Regression Modelling at Massey University."
---

# Preface {-}

These notes are the compiled lecture material for the course 161.251 Regression
Modelling taught at Massey University by Jonathan Godfrey (Weeks 1--8 and
appendices) and Nick Knowlton (Weeks 9--12).

We use R in almost every lecture; you should be able to run the code on your own.
The material makes use of `tidyverse` packages. Graphs are made using a mixture
of base R graphics and ggplot functions, with the choice being made on the basis
of convenience.

Please ask for help if you don't understand the R code being demonstrated.

```{r setup-book, include=FALSE}
source("../shared/setup.R")

knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  fig.path = "_main_files/figure-html/",
  cache.path = "../build/cache/book/"
)

# Write package bibliography
if (!file.exists("packages.bib")) {
  knitr::write_bib(file = "packages.bib")
}
```
